title: Check extcodesize to prevent silent success when calling EOA addresses
description: When call() targets an EOA (externally owned account), the EVM returns success=1 with returndatasize=0 — identical to a non-standard ERC20 that returns no data. extcodesize(token) distinguishes the two: an EOA has codesize 0, a deployed contract has codesize > 0. Without this check, transferring tokens to/from an EOA address would silently succeed without actually moving any tokens.
when_to_apply: Any low-level call to a token address that might be user-supplied
when_not_to_apply: When the token address is known to be a deployed contract (e.g., hardcoded WETH address)
